message:
test(ffs-dir): metamorphic property — swap_inode_in_entry is involutive

Add proptest_swap_inode_in_entry_is_involutive to pin the round-trip
contract: swapping a live entry's inode to a new value and then back
to the original must restore the block bytes exactly.

The VFS rename/unlink/link sequence relies on this round-trip — a
regression that altered any other byte of the entry header (file_type,
rec_len, name_len, name padding) would silently corrupt directory
metadata that downstream parse_dir_block consumers depend on. Existing
unit tests cover retarget_entry's combined inode+file_type swap but
no property test verified the bare swap_inode_in_entry path.

Strategy: init a directory block, add one entry, snapshot the bytes,
swap to a different inode, assert the bytes diverged, swap back to
the original, assert byte-for-byte equality with the snapshot.
